results.possibleIdentity: Dr. Jacques Habib was a chemical engineer active in the late 1960s and early 1970s. He earned a Doctorate (Ph.D.) in Physical Sciences from the University of Toulouse 3 in 1971, where his thesis focused on the filtration of pure water through clays and Darcy's Law ("L'écoulement de filtration de l'eau pure à travers les argiles et la loi de Darcy"). Professionally, he was associated with The Lummus Company, a global engineering and construction firm, and was based in Newark and Bloomfield, New Jersey, where the company's major engineering center was located. Dr. Habib was a technical member of Experiments in Art and Technology (E.A.T.), a non-profit organization that facilitated collaborations between artists and engineers. His E.A.T. index card identifies his employer as The Lummus Company and his technical expertise as chemical engineering.
